name: Henri J. M. Nouwen
bio: Dutch Catholic priest, professor, and writer whose work on spiritual life and pastoral care has shaped generations of clergy and chaplains.

name: Howard Thurman
bio: African American author, philosopher, theologian, and civil rights leader whose meditative writings remain foundational to Black church spirituality.

name: Brené Brown
bio: Research professor at the University of Houston known for her work on vulnerability, courage, shame, and empathy.

name: Barbara Kingsolver
bio: American novelist, essayist, and poet. Her fiction explores social justice, community, and the natural world with moral clarity and narrative grace.

name: Anne Tyler
bio: American novelist and Pulitzer Prize winner known for intimate portrayals of family life, memory, and the quiet weight of ordinary days.

name: Nadia Bolz-Weber
bio: Lutheran pastor and author whose writing on grace, failure, and community speaks honestly to people who have felt excluded from the church.

name: Frederick Buechner
bio: American writer, theologian, and ordained minister. His memoir and fiction blend faith with acute psychological honesty.

name: Marilynne Robinson
bio: American novelist and essayist whose fiction meditates on grace, death, and Calvinist American spirituality with luminous prose.

name: Kate Bowler
bio: Duke Divinity School professor and cancer survivor whose writing on suffering, faith, and prosperity theology resonates deeply with hospital chaplains.

name: Parker J. Palmer
bio: Quaker author, educator, and activist whose work on vocation, community, and contemplative living has shaped pastoral formation for decades.

name: Cole Arthur Riley
bio: Creator of Black Liturgies and author of This Here Flesh, exploring embodied spiritual practice rooted in Black culture and ancestral memory.

name: Walter Brueggemann
bio: Old Testament scholar and theologian whose work on prophetic imagination and lament has been central to progressive Protestant pastoral theology.
